tours.title: Our Tours
excursion.title: Our Excursions
excursion.sidebar.title: Airport Transfer Services in Morocco
excursion.sidebar.intro: Travel in comfort and style with Amsirar Transport Touristique, your trusted partner for airport transfers across Morocco.
excursion.sidebar.details: We provide reliable, punctual, and private transfer services from major airports including Marrakech, Casablanca, Agadir, and Fes to your hotel or destination.
excursion.sidebar.conclusion: With Amsirar Transport Touristique, your Moroccan adventure begins the moment you land.
tours.sidebar.intro: Travel in comfort and style with Amsirar Transport Touristique, your trusted partner for airport transfers across Morocco. We provide reliable, punctual, and private transfer services from major airports including Marrakech, Casablanca, Agadir, and Fes to your hotel or destination.
tours.sidebar.details: Our modern, air-conditioned vehicles and professional drivers ensure a safe and stress-free journey, whether you're arriving for business or leisure. Forget the hassle of taxis or waiting lines — we'll be there on time to welcome you and take you wherever you need to go.
tours.sidebar.conclusion: With Amsirar Transport Touristique, your Moroccan adventure begins the moment you land.
excursion.list.ouzoud.title: Ouzoud Waterfalls Day Trip
excursion.list.ouzoud.description: Journey to Morocco's tallest waterfalls, meet playful macaques, and cool off by the cascades.
excursion.list.ouzoud.tagline: A refreshing escape into the Middle Atlas.
excursion.list.essaouira.title: Essaouira Coastal Escape
excursion.list.essaouira.description: Stroll the fortified medina, watch blue fishing boats in the harbor, and enjoy ocean-fresh seafood.
excursion.list.imlil.title: Imlil & Toubkal Trek
excursion.list.imlil.description: Hike through terraced villages, share mint tea with locals, and admire views of Mount Toubkal.
excursion.list.ourika.title: Ourika Valley Discovery
excursion.list.ourika.description: Explore Berber villages, visit an argan cooperative, and hike to the Setti Fatma waterfalls.
excursion.list.ourika.tagline: Green valleys and cool mountain air.
excursion.list.agafay.title: Agafay Desert Sunset
excursion.list.agafay.description: Ride camels or quads across the stony dunes, then dine under the stars with live music.
excursion.list.agafay.tagline: Adventure and dinner beneath desert skies.
excursion.list.balloon.title: Sunrise Hot Air Balloon
excursion.list.balloon.alt: Hot air balloon floating at sunrise
excursion.list.balloon.description: Lift off at dawn for sweeping views of Marrakech, then savor a Berber breakfast after landing.
excursion.list.balloon.tagline: A peaceful flight above the Atlas foothills.
excursion1.title: Ouzoud Waterfalls Day Trip
excursion1.highlights: Admire the 110-meter cascades from multiple viewpoints.
Follow a guided trail through olive groves and Berber villages.
Spot playful Barbary macaques along the canyon.
Upgrade with an optional boat ride at the base of the falls.
excursion1.overview: Escape Marrakech for a refreshing day at Ouzoud Waterfalls. Travel with a local guide, trek between viewpoints, meet resident macaques, and cool off with an optional boat ride before a riverside lunch.
excursion1.bookingTitle: Reserve Your Ouzoud Waterfalls Day Trip
excursion2.title: Essaouira Coastal Escape
excursion2.alt: Essaouira harbor with blue fishing boats
excursion2.highlights: Stroll the UNESCO-listed medina and its blue-and-white alleys.
Watch traditional blue boats unload the day's catch in the harbor.
Relax on the wide sandy beach with refreshing Atlantic breezes.
Taste freshly caught seafood at a trusted local restaurant.
excursion2.overview: Discover the laid-back charm of Essaouira. Wander its artisan souks, explore the ramparts, feel the ocean breeze on the beach, and enjoy a seafood feast before returning to Marrakech at sunset.
excursion3.title: Imlil & Toubkal Adventure
excursion3.alt: View toward Mount Toubkal from Imlil
excursion3.highlights: Journey through Atlas mountain villages with a local guide.
Enjoy panoramic views of Mount Toubkal from scenic trails.
Share mint tea with a Berber family and learn about daily life.
Choose between a gentle hike or a mule ride to suit your pace.
excursion3.overview: Head into the High Atlas for an immersive day in Imlil and Mount Toubkal. Follow scenic trails, meet Berber hosts, and savor a home-cooked lunch overlooking terraced valleys.
excursion4.title: Ourika Valley Discovery
excursion4.alt: Traveler walking beside the Ourika River
excursion4.highlights: Visit Berber villages and their lively weekly souks.
Stop at an argan oil cooperative run by local women.
Hike with a guide to the Setti Fatma waterfalls.
Enjoy a riverside tagine lunch with Atlas views.
excursion4.overview: Swap the city bustle for the lush Ourika Valley. Journey along the Atlas foothills, learn about Berber traditions, hike to waterfalls, and relax beside the river for a fresh tagine.
excursion5.title: Agafay Desert Sunset Experience
excursion5.alt: Dinner setup in the Agafay Desert at dusk
excursion5.highlights: Ride a camel or quad bike across the stony Agafay dunes.
Watch the sun set over rolling desert hills.
Savor a candlelit Moroccan dinner under the stars.
Listen to live Gnawa rhythms around the campfire.
excursion5.overview: Experience the rugged beauty of the Agafay Desert. Combine adventure with a golden-hour sunset, a festive dinner, and traditional music in an intimate desert camp.
excursion6.title: Sunrise Hot Air Balloon Ride
excursion6.alt: Hot air balloon drifting above Marrakech
excursion6.highlights: Glide above palm groves, villages, and the Atlas foothills.
Capture the sunrise from a hot air balloon basket.
Enjoy a Berber breakfast served in a desert tent.
Receive shared return transfers and a flight certificate.
excursion6.overview: Lift off before dawn for a sunrise hot air balloon ride above Marrakech. Watch the landscape glow beneath you, celebrate with mint tea, and savor a Berber breakfast after landing.
about.title: About Us
contact.title: Contact Us
home.heroSubtitle: Specialist in Morocco travel and tours, Sahara desert trips, and excursions to the imperial cities of Morocco.
about.whyTrustTitle: Why travelers trust our 20 years of Amsirar experience:
about.whyTrust.point1: Deep local knowledge and cultural connection
about.whyTrust.point3: Tailored itineraries for hiking, camping, or cultural immersion
about.whyTrust.point4: Proven reputation built on excellence and authenticity
about.experienceText1: For over two decades, we have guided travelers through the breathtaking landscapes of Amsirar, sharing the region's hidden beauty, culture, and timeless traditions. With 20 years of experience, our journeys have become more than just trips — they are immersive stories woven through the High Atlas Mountains.
about.experienceText2: From the serene valleys and ancient Berber villages to the winding mountain trails and star-filled desert skies, we have perfected every detail to offer our guests authentic, safe, and unforgettable experiences.
about.experienceText3: Our seasoned local guides know every path, every story, and every smile along the way. Whether you seek adventure, cultural discovery, or peaceful retreat, our 20 years of expertise ensure a personalized journey shaped by passion, trust, and deep respect for Amsirar's natural and cultural heritage.
about.conclusionText: Come experience Amsirar not just as a visitor, but as a friend guided by 20 years of dedication, discovery, and love for this extraordinary land.
about.facts.smTitle: know some facts about our agency
contact.headerSubtitle:
Ready to get our best services!
Feel free to contact us!
contact.form.title: Send us a message
tours.comments.title: Comments
tours.comments.form.name: Name *
tours.detailsSubtitle: Stories, tips, and insights from our adventures
tours.reservation.title: Make Reservation
tour1.title: Desert Tour from Marrakech to Merzouga
tour1.day1: Day 1: Marrakech ➜ Ait Ben Haddou ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Dades Gorges
tour1.day2: Day 2: Dades ➜ Todra Gorges ➜ Merzouga (Camel Trek & Desert Camp)
tour1.overview: Embark on a breathtaking 3-day adventure from Marrakech to the heart of the Sahara Desert. Experience the magic of the High Atlas Mountains, the Kasbahs of Ouarzazate, and the golden dunes of Merzouga. Ride a camel into the sunset, spend the night under a star-filled sky, and discover the authentic beauty of Morocco.
tour1.day1.text: Departure early morning from Marrakech, crossing the High Atlas Mountains via the Tizi n’Tichka Pass (2,260m).
Visit the UNESCO Kasbah of Ait Ben Haddou, famous for its appearance in many films such as Gladiator and Game of Thrones.
Continue to Ouarzazate, known as the "Hollywood of Africa".
Drive through the Skoura Oasis and Valley of Roses to reach Dades Gorges.
Dinner and overnight in a traditional riad or hotel in Dades Valley.
tour1.day2.text: After breakfast, journey to the spectacular Todra Gorges, a favorite for climbers and photographers.
Continue through Tinejdad and Erfoud, known for fossil workshops and date markets.
Arrive in Merzouga, where your camel trek begins.
Ride into the Erg Chebbi dunes and watch a magical sunset.
Arrive at your desert camp, enjoy Berber music by the fire, and sleep under the stars or in a luxury tent.
tour1.day3.text: After breakfast, depart from the desert camp and head towards the Draa Valley.
Visit the stunning Ksar of Ait Ben Haddou, a UNESCO World Heritage site.
Continue through the Ourika Valley, known for its waterfalls and Berber villages.
Arrive back in Marrakech in the evening, where your adventure concludes.
tour1.includes:
Transportation in modern air-conditioned 4x4 or minibus
English / French / Spanish-speaking driver-guide
1 night in Dades Valley (hotel or riad)
1 night in desert camp (standard or luxury)
Camel ride in Merzouga dunes
Breakfasts & dinners as mentioned
Sandboarding (optional)
tour1.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Entrance fees (if applicable)
Tips & personal expenses
tour1.goodToKnow:
Departure: 7:00 AM from Marrakech
Return: Evening of Day 3
Luggage: You can bring your small suitcase or backpack on the camel
Bring sunglasses, hat, sunscreen, and warm clothes for the desert night
tour2.overview: This 6-day journey takes you through Morocco’s most iconic cities and breathtaking landscapes. From the cosmopolitan energy of Casablanca to the blue charm of Chefchaouen, the ancient medina of Fes, and the golden dunes of the Sahara — experience Morocco’s culture, colors, and traditions in one unforgettable adventure.
tour2.day1.text: Pick-up from your hotel or the airport in Casablanca.
Visit Hassan II Mosque, the largest in Morocco, standing proudly by the Atlantic Ocean.
Drive to Rabat, Morocco's capital, and explore Hassan Tower, Mohamed V Mausoleum, and the Kasbah of the Udayas.
Continue north through the Rif Mountains to reach the stunning Blue City of Chefchaouen.
Free evening to explore the blue-painted streets and relax in the medina.
tour2.day2.text: Morning exploration of Chefchaouen's blue-washed buildings, waterfalls, and souks.
Drive to Fes, Morocco's spiritual capital and oldest imperial city.
Afternoon visit to the medina, a UNESCO World Heritage site, with its labyrinthine alleys, tanneries, and artisan workshops.
Dinner and overnight in a traditional riad in Fes.
tour2.day3.text: Full day dedicated to Fes: Visit the University of Al Quaraouiyine, the oldest continuously operating university in the world.
Explore the Jewish Quarter (Mellah), the Royal Palace, and the Saadian Tombs.
Afternoon free for shopping or relaxing.
tour2.day4.text: Depart Fes and drive through the Middle Atlas Mountains to Ifrane, Morocco's 'Little Switzerland.'
Continue to Azrou, known for its cedar forests and Barbary macaques.
Arrive in Midelt, a gateway to the desert, and explore the area.
tour2.day5.text: Drive through the Ziz Valley with its dramatic gorges and palm groves.
Arrive in Merzouga and enjoy a camel trek into the Erg Chebbi dunes.
Watch a magical sunset, enjoy Berber music around the campfire, and sleep under the stars in a desert camp.
tour2.day6.text: Early morning sunrise over the dunes.
After breakfast, drive back through Erfoud and the Draa Valley.
Cross the High Atlas Mountains to return to Marrakech.
Arrival in Marrakech in the evening.
tour2.includes:
Transportation in modern air-conditioned 4x4 or minibus
English / French / Spanish-speaking driver-guide
5 nights accommodation (hotels/riads/desert camp)
Breakfasts & dinners as mentioned
Camel ride in Merzouga dunes
All entrance fees and activities
tour2.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
International flights
Tips & personal expenses
tour2.goodToKnow:
Departure: Daily from Casablanca
Return: Day 6 evening to Marrakech
Luggage: 1 suitcase per person
Bring comfortable walking shoes, hat, and sunscreen
tour2.title: Tour from Casablanca to Marrakech
tour2.day4: Day 4: Fes ➜ Midelt ➜ Ziz Valley ➜ Merzouga Desert
tour2.day5: Day 5: Merzouga ➜ Todra Gorges ➜ Dades Valley
tour2.day6: Day 6: Dades Valley ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Ait Ben Haddou ➜ Marrakech
tour3.overview:
Embark on an unforgettable 3-day journey from Fes to Marrakech through the mesmerizing landscapes of southern Morocco. Travel across the Middle Atlas Mountains, ride camels into the Merzouga Desert, spend a magical night under the stars, and explore the Kasbahs and valleys on your way to Marrakech.
This tour combines adventure, culture, and scenic beauty - the perfect Moroccan experience.
tour3.day1: Day 1: Fes ➜ Ifrane ➜ Azrou ➜ Midelt ➜ Ziz Valley ➜ Merzouga Desert
tour3.day2: Day 2: Merzouga ➜ Todra Gorges ➜ Dades Valley
tour3.day3: Day 3: Dades Valley ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Ait Ben Haddou ➜ Marrakech
tour3.day1.text: Pick-up from your hotel in Fes early morning.
Travel through the Middle Atlas Mountains, stopping at Ifrane, known as the "Switzerland of Morocco."
Stop at the Cedar Forest of Azrou to see the playful Barbary monkeys.
Continue south to Midelt for lunch.
Drive through the Ziz Valley with its palm groves and kasbahs.
Arrive in Merzouga in the afternoon, where your camel trek awaits.
Ride across the Erg Chebbi dunes, enjoy the sunset, and spend the night in a desert camp under a starlit sky.
tour3.day2.text: Wake early to watch the sunrise over the dunes — a truly unforgettable moment.
Return by camel to the village of Merzouga for breakfast and a refreshing shower.
Drive to Tinghir and explore the breathtaking Todra Gorges, Morocco's most famous canyon.
Walk between its towering cliffs (up to 300m high).
Continue to the beautiful Dades Valley, known for its rock formations and Kasbahs.
Dinner and overnight stay in a hotel overlooking the valley.
tour3.day3.text: After breakfast, continue through the Valley of Roses and Skoura Oasis to Ouarzazate, known as the "Hollywood of Africa."
Visit Kasbah Ait Ben Haddou, a UNESCO World Heritage site and filming location for Gladiator and Game of Thrones.
Cross the High Atlas Mountains via the Tizi n'Tichka Pass (2,260m) with panoramic views.
Arrive in Marrakech in the evening, drop-off at your accommodation.
tour3.includes:
Pick-up in Fes & drop-off in Marrakech
Private transport in air-conditioned 4x4 or minibus
English/French/Spanish-speaking driver-guide
1 night in Dades Valley (hotel/riad)
1 night in desert camp (standard or luxury)
Camel ride in Merzouga dunes
Breakfasts & dinners as mentioned
Sandboarding (optional)
tour3.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Entrance fees (if applicable)
Tips & personal expenses
tour3.goodToKnow:
Departure: 7:30 AM from Fes
End: Marrakech (evening of Day 3)
Luggage: You can bring a small suitcase or backpack on the camel
Bring: Sunglasses, sunscreen, hat, and warm clothes (nights in the desert can be cold)
Available: Daily, all year round
Customization: Upgrade to luxury desert camp or add extra nights on request
tour4.overview: Discover Morocco's northern gems on this 5-day journey from Casablanca to Fes. Explore the modern and historical highlights of Casablanca, the political capital Rabat, the picturesque Blue City of Chefchaouen, the ancient Roman ruins of Volubilis, and the imperial city of Meknes before reaching Fes, Morocco's spiritual and cultural capital. This tour offers a perfect balance of history, culture, and breathtaking scenery.
tour4.day4: Day 4: Full-Day Guided Tour of Fes
tour4.day1.text: Pick-up from Casablanca Mohammed V Airport or your hotel.
Visit Hassan II Mosque, one of the largest mosques in the world, located by the Atlantic Ocean.
Explore the Corniche Ain Diab and the Old Medina of Casablanca.
Free evening to relax or enjoy dinner in a local restaurant.
tour4.day2.text: After breakfast, departure to Rabat, Morocco's capital.
Visit the Royal Palace, Hassan Tower, and the Mausoleum of Mohammed V.
Explore the Kasbah of the Udayas and stroll along the Bou Regreg River.
Continue to Chefchaouen, the famous Blue City nestled in the Rif Mountains.
Evening free to wander the blue-painted streets and enjoy the relaxed atmosphere.
tour4.day3.text: Morning at leisure to capture more photos in Chefchaouen.
Depart for the ancient Roman ruins of Volubilis (UNESCO World Heritage Site).
Continue to Meknes, one of Morocco's imperial cities, to visit Bab Mansour Gate, El Hedim Square, and Moulay Ismail Mausoleum.
tour4.day4.text: After breakfast, meet your local guide for a full exploration of Fes el Bali, the world's oldest living medieval city.
Visit Al-Qarawiyyin University, Bou Inania Madrasa, Nejjarine Fountain, and the colorful Chouara Tanneries.
Explore traditional markets, artisan workshops, and panoramic viewpoints.
tour4.day5.text: Depending on your schedule, transfer to Fes Airport or Casablanca Airport.
End of the tour with wonderful memories of Morocco!
tour4.includes:
Private transportation in air-conditioned 4x4 or minibus
English / French / Spanish-speaking driver-guide
4 nights accommodation (hotels/riads)
Local guide in Fes
Breakfasts as mentioned
Airport transfers (Casablanca & Fes)
tour4.excludes:
Lunches & dinners not mentioned
Entrance fees
Tips & personal expenses
tour4.goodToKnow:
Departure: Casablanca (hotel or airport)
End: Fes or Casablanca
Duration: 5 Days / 4 Nights
Availability: Daily departures
Customization: Add nights, upgrade hotels, or continue the tour to the Sahara Desert
tour5.overview: This 10-day tour from Marrakech to Casablanca lets you experience the best of Morocco — from the Sahara Desert dunes to the imperial cities, Atlas Mountains, and Atlantic coast.
tour5.day3: Day 3: Marrakech ➜ Ait Ben Haddou ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Dades Valley
tour5.day4: Day 4: Dades Valley ➜ Todra Gorges ➜ Merzouga Desert
tour5.day5: Day 5: Merzouga ➜ Ziz Valley ➜ Midelt ➜ Fes
tour5.day1.text: Pick-up from Marrakech Airport and transfer to your riad/hotel.
Free time to explore the Jemaa el-Fna Square.
tour5.day2.text: Guided tour: Koutoubia, Bahia Palace, Saadian Tombs.
Explore the Medina souks; optional Majorelle Garden.
tour5.day3.text: Cross High Atlas via Tizi n'Tichka Pass.
Visit Ait Ben Haddou (UNESCO) and Ouarzazate.
tour5.day4.text: Walk in Todra Gorges, then continue to Merzouga.
Camel trek into Erg Chebbi and desert camp night.
tour5.day5.text: Sunrise over dunes, breakfast, then travel via Ziz Valley.
Cross Middle Atlas (Midelt, Azrou Cedar Forest, Ifrane).
tour5.day6.text: Full day exploring Fes, the oldest imperial city in Morocco.
Visit the medina, tanneries, and University of Al Quaraouiyine.
tour5.day7.text: Drive to Meknes, known as the 'Versailles of Morocco'.
Visit Volubilis Roman ruins, then continue to Chefchaouen.
Explore the blue-washed streets of Chefchaouen.
tour5.day8.text: Travel from Chefchaouen to Rabat, Morocco's capital.
Visit Hassan Tower, Mohamed V Mausoleum, and Kasbah of the Udayas.
tour5.day9.text: Drive to Casablanca, Morocco's economic hub.
Visit Hassan II Mosque and explore the modern city.
tour5.day10.text: Transfer to Casablanca Mohammed V Airport for departure.
tour5.includes:
Private transport in air-conditioned 4x4 or minibus
English / French / Spanish-speaking driver-guide
9 nights accommodation (hotels, riads, desert camp)
Camel trek in Merzouga Desert
Local guides in Marrakech & Fes
Breakfasts and dinners as mentioned
tour5.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Entrance fees (if applicable)
Tips & personal expenses
tour5.goodToKnow:
Departure: Marrakech (airport or hotel)
End: Casablanca (airport or hotel)
Duration: 10 Days / 9 Nights
Available: Daily, all year round
tour6.overview:
This 4-day adventure from Marrakech to Merzouga Desert lets you experience Morocco's stunning landscapes — from the High Atlas Mountains and ancient kasbahs to the Sahara Desert dunes.
Enjoy camel trekking, desert camping, Berber music under the stars, and charming valleys along the way.
tour6.day1: Day 1: Marrakech ➜ Ait Ben Haddou ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Dades Valley
tour6.day2: Day 2: Dades Valley ➜ Todra Gorges ➜ Merzouga Desert
tour6.day3: Day 3: Merzouga ➜ Alnif ➜ Draa Valley ➜ Ouarzazate
tour6.day4: Day 4: Ouarzazate ➜ High Atlas Mountains ➜ Marrakech
tour6.day1.text: Cross the High Atlas Mountains via the Tizi n'Tichka Pass (2,260m), with panoramic stops for photos.
Visit the Kasbah of Ait Ben Haddou, a UNESCO World Heritage site featured in many movies (Gladiator, Game of Thrones).
Continue to Ouarzazate, known as the "Hollywood of Africa."
Drive through the Valley of Roses and Skoura Oasis to reach Dades Valley.
tour6.day2.text: Breakfast at the hotel, then drive to the impressive Todra Gorges, where you can walk between 300m cliffs.
Continue through Tinejdad and Erfoud, known for fossils and dates.
Arrive at Merzouga, gateway to the Sahara Desert.
Meet your camel caravan and ride across the Erg Chebbi dunes to your desert camp.
Enjoy a traditional dinner, Berber drumming, and a night under the stars.
tour6.day3.text: Wake early to catch the sunrise over the dunes — a breathtaking moment.
Ride camels or drive back to the village for breakfast and a shower.
Depart through Alnif and Tazzarine, following the scenic Draa Valley, one of Morocco's longest river valleys.
Continue toward Ouarzazate, known for its film studios and kasbahs.
tour6.day4.text: After breakfast, visit the Taourirt Kasbah or Atlas Film Studios (optional).
Return journey to Marrakech through the High Atlas Mountains.
Arrive in Marrakech by late afternoon.
tour6.includes:
Pick-up and drop-off in Marrakech
Private transport in air-conditioned 4x4 or minibus
English / French / Spanish-speaking driver-guide
2 nights in traditional riads/hotels
1 night in desert camp (standard or luxury)
Camel trek in Merzouga dunes
Breakfasts & dinners (as mentioned)
Sandboarding (optional)
tour6.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Entrance fees (if applicable)
Tips & personal expenses
tour6.goodToKnow:
Departure: 7:30 AM from Marrakech
End: Marrakech (evening of Day 4)
Distance: ~1,000 km total
Bring: Sunglasses, hat, sunscreen, and warm clothes for desert nights
Customization: Can start or end in Fes / upgrade to luxury camp available
tour7.overview: Explore Morocco's diverse landscapes on this 4-day adventure from Agadir to Marrakech. Journey through the Anti-Atlas Mountains, the valleys and kasbahs of Dades, and the Sahara Desert dunes at Merzouga. Ride camels, witness desert sunsets and sunrises, and experience traditional Berber hospitality.
tour7.day1: Day 1: Agadir ➜ Taroudant ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Dades Valley
tour7.day2: Day 2: Dades Valley ➜ Todra Gorges ➜ Merzouga Desert
tour7.day3: Day 3: Merzouga ➜ Alnif ➜ Draa Valley ➜ Ouarzazate
tour7.day4: Day 4: Ouarzazate ➜ High Atlas Mountains ➜ Marrakech
tour7.day1.text: Drive through Taroudant, known as the "Little Marrakech," with its red walls and souks.
Continue through the Anti-Atlas Mountains toward Ouarzazate.
Pass through Skoura Oasis and the Valley of Roses to reach Dades Valley.
tour7.day2.text: After breakfast, visit Todra Gorges, towering cliffs ideal for walking and photos.
Continue toward Merzouga, passing Tinejdad and Erfoud, famous for dates and fossils.
Arrive in Merzouga and start a camel trek into the Erg Chebbi dunes.
Watch the sunset over the desert and enjoy a traditional Berber dinner around the campfire.
tour7.day3.text: Early wake-up to catch the sunrise over the dunes.
Return by camel or vehicle to Merzouga village for breakfast and shower.
Drive through Alnif and Tazzarine, following the scenic Draa Valley with its palm groves and kasbahs.
Continue to Ouarzazate, optional visit to Atlas Film Studios or Taourirt Kasbah.
tour7.day4.text: Cross the High Atlas Mountains via Tizi n'Tichka Pass (2,260m) with panoramic photo stops.
Arrive in Marrakech in the late afternoon, ending the tour.
tour7.includes:
Pickup & drop-off from Agadir/Marrakech
Private 4x4 or minibus transport
English/French-speaking driver-guide
1 night desert camp in Merzouga
2 nights hotels/riads (Dades Valley & Ouarzazate)
Camel trek in Merzouga dunes
Breakfasts & dinners as mentioned
tour7.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Entrance fees
Tips & personal expenses
tour7.goodToKnow:
Departure: 7:00–8:00 AM from Agadir
Duration: 4 Days / 3 Nights
Distance: ~900 km total
Bring: Sunglasses, hat, sunscreen, warm clothing for desert nights, camera
Optional: Sandboarding, extra night in desert camp, photography tour
tour8.overview: Escape into the Sahara on this 3-day journey from Marrakech to the remote Chegaga dunes. Cross the High Atlas Mountains, explore ancient kasbahs, ride camels into the sunset, and spend a night under the stars in a desert camp.
tour8.day1: Day 1: Marrakech ➜ Ait Ben Haddou ➜ Ouarzazate ➜ Zagora
tour8.day1.text: Cross the High Atlas Mountains via the Tizi n'Tichka Pass (2,260m).
Visit Ait Ben Haddou Kasbah, a UNESCO World Heritage Site and famous movie location.
Continue to Ouarzazate, the 'Hollywood of Africa,' and then drive to Zagora via Draa Valley, with its lush palm groves and traditional villages.
tour8.day2.text: Early breakfast, then continue south through the desert plains to M'Hamid, the last village before the dunes.
Board camels for a ride into the Chegaga Desert, witnessing the sunset over endless golden dunes.
Enjoy traditional Berber dinner around the campfire, stargazing, and overnight stay in a desert camp.
tour8.day3.text: Wake up early for a spectacular sunrise over the dunes.
Return by camel to M'Hamid village for breakfast and a short rest.
Drive back through Draa Valley, visiting kasbahs and palm groves.
Cross the High Atlas Mountains again to reach Marrakech in the evening.
tour8.includes:
Pickup & drop-off in Marrakech
Private 4x4 or minibus transport
English/French-speaking driver-guide
1 night desert camp in Chegaga
1 night hotel in Zagora
Camel trek in Chegaga dunes
Breakfasts & dinners as mentioned
tour8.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Entrance fees
Tips & personal expenses
tour8.goodToKnow:
Departure: 7:00–8:00 AM from Marrakech
Duration: 3 Days / 2 Nights
Distance: ~500 km each way
Bring: Warm clothes (desert nights can be cold), sunglasses, hat, sunscreen, camera
Optional: Sandboarding, extra nights in desert camp, photography sessions
tour9.overview: Embark on a captivating 7-day journey through North Morocco, from the vibrant coastal city of Tangier to the enchanting imperial city of Marrakech. Discover ancient Roman ruins, imperial palaces, the mystical Blue City of Chefchaouen, and the bustling souks of Fes and Marrakech. This tour offers a perfect blend of history, culture, and natural beauty.
tour9.day1.text: Arrival in Tangier ➜ City Highlights Tour
Pick-up from Tangier Airport/Port (Tanger Med or city port).
Visit Cap Spartel, where the Mediterranean meets the Atlantic.
Explore the Kasbah, old Medina, and Petit Socco.
Walk along Tangier’s Corniche and beach promenade.
tour9.day2.text: Continue to Chefchaouen, the famous Blue Pearl.
Explore the old medina, Outa El Hammam square, Ras El Ma river, and artisan shops.
Sunset walk to the Spanish Mosque viewpoint.
tour9.day3.text: Drive to the Roman ruins of Volubilis (guided visit available).
Continue to Meknes, a Moroccan imperial city: Bab Mansour, Sahrij Swani, Old Medina.
Arrival in Fes in the evening.
tour9.day4.text: Full Day Exploring Fes ➜ Imperial, Spiritual & Cultural Capital

Guided tour with a certified local guide: Fes el-Bali (oldest part of the city), Al-Qarawiyyin University (the world’s oldest), Tannery of Chouara, Bou Inania and Attarine Madrasas, Nejjarine Square and Museum, Pottery and zellige workshops.
Free evening to relax or enjoy a traditional Moroccan dinner show.
tour9.day5.text: Fes ➜ Ifrane ➜ Azrou ➜ Beni Mellal ➜ Marrakech
Drive through the Middle Atlas Mountains.
Visit the Cedar Forest of Azrou to see the wild Barbary macaques.
tour9.day6.text: Guided city tour: Koutoubia Mosque (exterior), Bahia Palace, Saadian Tombs, Jewish Quarter (Mellah), Medina & Souks, Jemaa El Fna Square.
Optional afternoon visit to Majorelle Garden, Yves Saint Laurent Museum.
tour9.day7.text: Free time depending on your flight schedule.
tour9.includes:
Private transportation in a modern A/C vehicle
English/French/Spanish-speaking driver
6 nights accommodation (Hotels/Riads)
Daily breakfast
Tangier airport/port pickup
Marrakech airport drop-off
Local guide in Fes
Local guide in Marrakech
Stops for photos, panoramic views, breaks, and sightseeing
Fuel & taxes
tour9.excludes:
Lunches & drinks
Dinners (unless your package includes some)
Entrance fees to monuments
Tips for driver or local guides
Personal expenses
Optional activities (e.g., Hammam, cooking class, museums, etc.)
tour9.goodToKnow:
This tour is private, so you can stop anywhere along the way.
Riads in old medinas may require short walking distances.
Comfortable walking shoes are recommended.
Chefchaouen & Fes have many uphill/downhill alleys.
The weather in the north can be cooler, especially in winter.
You can customize the tour: add days, upgrade hotels, add desert extension.
Vegetarian/vegan meals available upon request.
Luggage is always safe in the car during visits.
tour3.title: Desert Tour from Fes to Marrakech
tour4.title: Tour from Casablanca to Fes
tour5.title: Tour from Marrakech to Casablanca
tour6.title: Desert Tour from Marrakech via Merzouga Desert
tour7.title: 4 Days Tour from Agadir to Marrakech via Merzouga Desert
tour8.title: 3 Days Marrakech to Chegaga Desert Tour
tours.tour1.description: Sahara Magic: Ride camels across golden dunes, camp under a blanket of stars, and discover the enchanting beauty of Morocco's desert wilderness!
tours.tour2.description: Morocco Odyssey: Discover Casablanca's vibrant energy, Chefchaouen's mystical blues, Fes's ancient labyrinth, and Sahara's golden dunes in an epic 6-day cultural adventure!
tours.tour3.description: Marrakech Marvel: Wander bustling souks, explore stunning palaces, and soak in the vibrant culture of Morocco's red city!
tours.tour4.description: Northern Treasures: From Casablanca's modernity to Fes's ancient wonders, explore Morocco's north in a 5-day cultural journey!
tours.tour5.description: Morocco Wonders: Traverse Sahara dunes, imperial cities, Atlas peaks, and coastal elegance in a 10-day epic adventure!
tours.tour6.description: Desert Thrill: Conquer Atlas Mountains, trek Sahara dunes, and camp under stars in a 4-day Merzouga adventure!
tours.tour7.description: Merzouga Desert Journey: 4-day adventure from Agadir to Marrakech through Anti-Atlas Mountains, Dades valleys, and Sahara dunes with camel treks and Berber culture!
tours.tour8.description: Chegaga Desert Escape: 3-day trip from Marrakech to remote dunes, crossing Atlas Mountains, exploring kasbahs, and stargazing in the Sahara!
tour9.title: 7 Days North Morocco Tour from Tangier to Marrakech
tours.tour9.description: North Morocco Odyssey: 7-day journey from Tangier to Marrakech exploring imperial cities, blue villages, Roman ruins, and vibrant souks!
featured.smTitle: know about some places before your travel
featured.cards.marrakech.title: Marrakech
featured.cards.marrakech.alt: Marrakech city view with traditional architecture
featured.marrakech.description: The Red City is Morocco's cultural gem, full of life, colors, and history. Explore Jemaa El-Fna, vibrant souks, and stunning gardens and palaces. A perfect mix of tradition and modern energy.
featured.cards.casablanca.title: Casablanca
featured.cards.casablanca.alt: Casablanca modern cityscape with Hassan II Mosque
featured.casablanca.description: Morocco's modern and cosmopolitan hub, famous for the stunning Hassan II Mosque by the sea. Enjoy its beachfront Corniche, stylish cafés, and art-deco buildings.
featured.cards.fez.title: Fes
featured.cards.fez.alt: Fes ancient medina with traditional Moroccan architecture
featured.fez.description: The spiritual and cultural heart of Morocco. Its ancient medina, tanneries, and Al Quaraouiyine University make it a living museum of Moroccan heritage.
featured.cards.dadesValley.title: Dades Valley
featured.cards.dadesValley.alt: Dades Valley scenic landscape with red rock canyons
featured.dadesValley.description: A dramatic valley with red rock canyons, ancient kasbahs, and scenic mountain roads. Ideal for hiking, photography, and discovering Berber villages.
featured.cards.chefchaouen.title: Chefchaouen
featured.cards.chefchaouen.alt: Chefchaouen blue city streets in the mountains
featured.chefchaouen.description: The Blue City nestled in the Rif Mountains. Famous for its blue-painted streets, calm vibe, and stunning mountain views — perfect for relaxing and taking photos.
featured.cards.rabat.title: Rabat
featured.cards.rabat.alt: Rabat capital city with modern and historical buildings
featured.rabat.description: The capital city of Morocco, combining modern life with history. Visit Hassan Tower, Kasbah of the Udayas, and enjoy the peaceful Atlantic coast atmosphere.
services.smTitle: know about services that we offer
services.cards.handcrafted.title: Handcrafted Experiences
services.cards.handcrafted.text: Experience the best of Morocco with our handcrafted tours, designed to showcase the country's rich culture and stunning landscapes.
services.cards.guide.title: Travel Guide
services.cards.guide.text: Visiting Morocco with an experienced travel guide can greatly enhance your experience, providing insights into local culture and history.
services.cards.price.title: Suitable Price
services.cards.price.text: We offer competitive pricing on all our tours, ensuring great value for an unforgettable experience.
testimonials.smTitle: what our clients say about us

kevin.name: Kevin Wilson
kevin.quote: I’ve traveled with several agencies before, but Amsirar Trip stands out for its attention to detail and authentic experiences. They don’t just take you to tourist spots — they make sure you experience the culture, the food, and the people. Our local guide was fantastic and made us feel like family. Everything ran smoothly, from airport pickup to our final day. Highly recommended for anyone who wants a personal touch to their travel plans.

ben.name: Ben Smith
ben.quote: Booking with Amsirar Trip was the best choice I made this year. The communication was fast, clear, and friendly from start to finish. The itinerary was well-balanced — adventure, relaxation, and exploration all in one. The guides were knowledgeable and always ready to help. What really stood out was how much effort they put into making sure everyone felt comfortable and safe throughout the journey. I’m already planning my next trip with them!

sara.name: Sara Garcia
sara.quote:
<montserrat>A Perfectly Organized Adventure!</montserrat>
My trip with Amsirar Trip was absolutely flawless. From the moment I booked, the team handled everything with care — hotel reservations, local transport, and guided tours. What impressed me most was how flexible they were when I wanted to adjust my itinerary. Every destination felt well thought-out and perfectly timed. I didn't have to worry about a single thing. It was the most stress-free vacation I've ever had!
